idea: Build CoverageCanvas, a shift coverage planner that assigns fictional field technicians to zones while enforcing availability, double-booking, and required-certification constraints.
audience: Field-service dispatchers planning a daily technician shift
outcome: Dispatchers can fill uncovered work safely, understand assignment conflicts, and undo changes without losing the original plan.
constraints: Local-only with deterministic fictional data and no backend, auth, database, analytics, APIs, or network calls
The coverage matrix must remain usable on narrow screens
Keyboard operable with visible focus and text explanations for every status
must_have_capabilities: Show shift-by-zone coverage requirements and current assignments
Assign an available technician to an uncovered role
Prevent double-booking and certification mismatches with specific explanations
Update filled and uncovered totals after a valid assignment
Undo the latest valid assignment and reset the full plan
visual_direction: A responsive dispatch canvas with a legible matrix, clear people-and-skill labels, and excellent negative-state feedback.
demo_preferences: Show an uncovered certified role, attempt an invalid assignment and explain it, make a valid assignment, show totals improving, undo, then reset.
